raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/2023, I purchased items from XXXX worth {$1700.00} and received my order on XX/XX/2023. In XXXX, upon inspecting the items, I found they were all defective because they weren't sewn properly, it's unsaleable. I contacted the merchant about the defects, informing them I could return the items as they offer a 30-day free return for defective items. The merchant ignored my request and insisted on remaking the items, but I declined, worried about a repeat issue. I took the matter to XXXX, but after almost a month, there's still no solution. I went to Citibank to dispute the charge. Despite providing all the necessary documents, Citibank credited me {$130.00}, which is not enough. I expect the full amount back, as I showed evidence of the defective products, evidence of the merchant ignoring my request to return the items, evidence that they offer a 30-day return, and more.

In Citibank 's first letter, they asked if I attempted to return the items, which I provided proof of my messages to the merchant that I want to return the items. However, I can't return the items if the merchant doesn't provide a return shipping label. How am I supposed to return them if I don't know where to ship them? 

Citibank 's last letter mentioned their attempt to resolve with the merchant, who still refuses to issue a credit.
